items.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic  Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
items.summary: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

items.title: FTC gives Musk the OK to acquire SpaceX alumni startup Mesh
items.summary: Mesh came out of stealth in February with a $50 million Series A.

items.title: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
items.summary: Corgi became embroiled in  controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.

items.title: Novak Djokovic has a new job —  advisor to private equity firm General Atlantic
items.summary: General Atlantic has tapped tennis legend Novak Djokovic to serve as a global strategic advisor.

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.summary: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

items.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
items.summary: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

items.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
items.summary: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]

items.title: Russian hackers were behind $2.5B hack of Jaguar Land Rover: Report
items.summary: The hack on car giant Jaguar Land Rover last year was one of the most disrupting, damaging, and costly hacks of the last few years.

items.title: Tesla settles FSD crash lawsuit as federal investigations continue
items.summary: The lawsuit was connected to a fatal 2023 crash involving a vehicle using the company's advanced driver assistance system known as Full Self-Driving.

items.title: TikTok’s road to becoming a super app
items.summary: TikTok may be working to become the app that people use for most of their digital activities.

items.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
items.summary: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

items.title: Xprize founder says ‘humans behave better when they’re being watched’
items.summary: Peter Diamandis is the latest tech executive to argue that global surveillance will make the world a better place, following Larry Ellison's comments in 2024.

items.title: Robotaxis drive miles just to get cleaned and charged; this new startup wants to fix that
items.summary: Aseon Labs, which came out of Y Combinator's 2026 spring cohort, has raised $10 million from Crane Venture Partners and others.

items.title: Early Bird pricing ends tonight for TechCrunch Founder Summit
items.summary: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026. Early Bird pricing ends today, at 11:59 p.m. PT, after which rates increase. Register now.

items.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
items.summary: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

items.title: YouTube Shorts are getting even shorter with an update that lets you double the playback speed
items.summary: YouTube Shorts is getting a makeover.

items.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
items.summary: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

items.title: Polymarket says hackers stole users’ funds
items.summary: The prediction market giant Polymarket said it's refunding users who had funds stolen due to a third-party breach.

items.title: Xbox follows Apple with price increases
items.summary: The company says the increases are being driven by rising memory and console storage prices, with costs more than 2.5x higher than previous levels.

items.title: Notion Mail shuts down amid agent takeover
items.summary: The company said it is discontinuing its email inbox in favor of its AI agent offering as users are increasingly handing over the reins of their email to the agents.
